title: Paavangalai Song Lyrics Taramani
stanzas:
Paavvangalai serthu
kodu engae selgirom
naam vazhndha vazhkai
puriyaamal
mannukkul sellugirom

enai mannippaayaa
yaah allaah
manam urugi
kaetkiren yaa allaah
illai sodhippaayaa
yaa allaah
neeye kooru allaah

arrahume arraheem
neethan yaa allaah
neethan yaa allaah

urakkam illai
irakkam kaattu
illai en valigalai
aattru
thavaru seithen
thavari seithen
karunaiyaalan neethan
allah.

Paavvangalai serthu
kodu engae selgirom
naam vazhndha vazhkai
puriyaamal
mannukkul sellugirom

ne irukkum idamthaan
edhu allah
adhu mannikkum maname
yaah allaah

un anbai pera vendugiren
ellam nee allaah
allam nee allaah

iraivaa un irakkathinaale
eeramugan irukkudhu
boomi.
erindhu mudindha
vilakkil indru
velicham ondru
vandhe olirgiradhe

unadhu adimai engu
povaen irundhu vitta
vaanathil vidiyal
neeye allaah

kaayangalai kattik kondu
unnidam vandhuvitten
en paavam yaavum
thooyavane engo
marandhuvitten

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Paavangalai?

The song conveys themes of longing and devotion, reflecting deep emotional sentiments.

What is the plot context of Taramani related to this song?

The song is intertwined with the emotional journey of the characters, highlighting their struggles and aspirations.

Who are the singers and music director of this song?

The song features the voices of Mukesh, Senthil Dass, and Yuvan, with music composed by Yuvan himself.

When was Paavangalai released?

The song was released on December 30, 2016.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Paavangalai, the characters of Taramani express their inner turmoil and yearning through a poignant melody. The song encapsulates the essence of devotion and emotional struggle, resonating deeply with listeners. With its soothing composition by Yuvan and heartfelt lyrics by Na Muthukumar, it evokes a sense of nostalgia and reflection. This track stands out as a beautiful representation of the film's themes, making it a memorable piece in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Paavangalai from Taramani is a soul-stirring melody that beautifully blends the voices of Mukesh, Senthil Dass, and Yuvan. This song captures the essence of longing and devotion, making it a must-listen for fans of heartfelt music. Experience the unique collaboration that brings a fresh perspective to Tamil cinema's musical landscape.
stanzaMeanings: We are living without understanding life, we are getting lost in the earth.
Are you going to forgive me, oh God? My heart melts, I am asking, oh God. Are you not going to help me, oh God? You are the one who guides, oh God.
Oh God, you are the merciful one, you are the compassionate one, oh God, you are the one, oh God.
You gave me life, what is the heart for? What is it for?
I am praying to you, you are the one who removes the sins.
There is no sleep, there is no rest. I have made mistakes in my paths, I have erred, oh God, you are the compassionate one.
We are living without understanding life, we are getting lost in the earth.
Are you going to forgive me, oh God?
The heart is like a crime that resides within.
In my heart, something is rolling and saying something.
Where you are, that is the place, oh God. That is the heart that will forgive, oh God.
I have fallen behind the big eagle, oh God.
I want to spread your love, everything is you, oh God, everything is you, oh God.
There is no one like you, oh God, your face is sweet, oh God.
Oh God, because of your mercy, the earth is filled with life. Today, a light has come from the extinguished lamp.
Holding the trembling hands, with compassion, remove the pain.
Where is your servitude going? From the sky where the dawn breaks, you are the one, oh God.
I have brought my burdens to you, my sins are all pure, I have forgotten everything.
